title: Goniopteris minor
intro: Goniopteris minor, also known as the minor chain fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine, particularly in the tropical regions of Central and South America, where it is valued for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits, including wound healing and the treatment of respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Goniopteris minor include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which have been shown to possess therapeutic properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Goniopteris minor is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and is also used in topical applications, such as creams and ointments, to leverage its medicinal properties.
taxonomy: Goniopteris minor is a species of fern in the family Dryopteridaceae. The genus Goniopteris is part of the order Cyatheales. It is also classified under the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns and their allies. Goniopteris minor is a relatively small species of fern that is found in tropical and subtropical regions. The classification of Goniopteris minor is used to understand its evolutionary relationships and place it within the broader plant kingdom.
